auth_notes: Authentication depends on whether the instance is on-premise (often Basic Auth or Windows Auth) or hosted via Trimble Transportation Cloud (OAuth2). On-premise setups frequently require a VPN or specific firewall rules to allow n8n access.
rate_limits: Varies by implementation and license; typically defined at the server level for on-premise installs.
gotchas: Legacy versions rely heavily on SOAP/XML, requiring complex payload construction in n8n.
Documentation is generally gated behind a customer support portal login.
Real-time data often requires polling or custom SQL triggers because native webhooks are rarely available.
client_must_provide: API Base URL (and VPN access if on-premise)
Trimble Cloud Credentials or API User credentials
Database schema access for custom integrations
research_notes: Integration will primarily use the HTTP Request node. For older TMW Suite versions, the user must manually construct SOAP envelopes (XML) and use the XML node to parse responses. Newer Trimble Transportation Cloud (TTC) endpoints are RESTful but require strict OAuth2 flows.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will primarily use the HTTP Request node. For older TMW Suite versions, the user must manually construct SOAP envelopes (XML) and use the XML node to parse responses. Newer Trimble Transportation Cloud (TTC) endpoints are RESTful but require strict OAuth2 flows.
